title: Register for Youth Achieving Results (Performing Arts)
description: Register a young performer for the Youth Achieving Results performing arts programme.
summary: Youth Achieving Results (YAR) supports young Barbadians in the arts. The Performing Arts strand is for people working in music, dance, theatre and spoken word.
You get classes with experienced practitioners, mentoring from established artists, and performance opportunities – sometimes at Ministry events, sometimes at festivals.
The programme is open to people aged 12 to 30. If you are under 16, a parent or guardian needs to register you.
There is no fee. You can include a performance reel or sample with your application if you have one, but it is not required.
who_can_apply: parents or guardians registering a child under 16

items: the participant’s full name, address and date of birth
the performing art they want to focus on (music, dance, theatre, spoken word)
their level of experience and any past performances
for under-16s, the parent or guardian’s details

how_long: Places are usually confirmed within 2 weeks of the closing date.

options.heading: Online
content: Complete the Youth Achieving Results (Performing Arts): Registration Form on the Ministry website.

contact.agency: Ministry of Youth, Sports and Community Empowerment
contact.address:
Sky Mall, Haggatt Hall
St. Michael

related.title: Register for Youth Achieving Results (Visual Arts)

assumptions: ‘Aged 12 to 30’ is the commonly-quoted eligibility range.
‘Places are usually confirmed within 2 weeks’ is an estimate.
Description of mentoring and performance opportunities is based on the programme’s general scope, not the form.
